phenotypes.label: Pituitary adenoma with hypercortisolism
phenotypes.description: Benign pituitary neoplasm with hypercortisolism, consistent with a pituitary adenoma/Cushing phenotype. Features include insulin resistance and metabolic changes (acanthosis nigricans, impaired fasting glycemia), proximal muscle weakness, and autonomic neuropathy, with co-occurring proteinuric kidney disease in some patients (nephritic/nephrotic syndromes).

phenotypes.label: GI symptoms with comorbidities
phenotypes.description: A nonspecific cluster of gastrointestinal and constitutional symptoms—nausea, vomiting, diarrhea/constipation, and abdominal pain—with dehydration and hypokalemia, alongside anemia. Common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, GERD, anxiety/depression, nicotine dependence) and general acute complaints (dyspnea, fever, chronic pain) dominate, representing a broad symptomatic baseline rather than a specific disease phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Testicular cancer with hypogonadism
phenotypes.description: Testicular malignancy with androgen deficiency, including testicular hypofunction/male hypogonadism and testicular pain. Associated findings include nodal involvement and malignancy-related venous thromboembolism, with some patients documented as status post orchiectomy.

phenotypes.label: MS/SCI with neurogenic bladder and ulcers
phenotypes.description: Patients show multiple sclerosis and spinal cord injury sequelae with neurogenic bladder and bowel, paraplegia or tetraplegia, and late effects of spinal cord damage. Complications include urinary retention and incontinence, recurrent urinary tract infection, pressure ulcers of the sacrum/buttock (often stage 3), and secondary osteomyelitis.

phenotypes.label: Prostate cancer and urinary complications
phenotypes.description: Prostate malignancy with prominent lower urinary tract symptoms and prostate-specific findings. Features include elevated PSA, benign prostatic hyperplasia, obstruction/retention, nocturia, hematuria, prostatitis, urethral stricture, and erectile dysfunction/male stress incontinence; urine cytology/microscopy abnormalities and radiation proctitis appear as related sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Venous thromboembolism and thrombophilia
phenotypes.description: Cluster of acute deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism, including pulmonary infarction. It co-occurs with hypercoagulable conditions such as hereditary thrombophilia and antiphospholipid syndrome/systemic lupus erythematosus, and with myeloproliferative neoplasms (polycythemia vera, essential thrombocythemia). Neuroendocrine malignancies also appear, consistent with cancer-associated thrombosis.

phenotypes.label: B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphomas, extranodal
phenotypes.description: Lymphoid malignancies dominated by B‑cell non‑Hodgkin lymphoma subtypes, including diffuse large B‑cell, follicular, and marginal zone/MALT lymphoma. Findings emphasize head and neck nodal disease and extranodal involvement, with frequent chronic sinonasal conditions suggesting local ENT involvement.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not form a consistent clinical pattern. They mix cardiopulmonary findings (effusions, valvular disease, arrhythmias) with unrelated problems (hydronephrosis, abdominal and GI symptoms), so there is no single unifying diagnosis to name.

phenotypes.label: Crohn's disease and sickle cell
phenotypes.description: This combines Crohn's disease (including small and large bowel involvement and anal fistula) with sickle cell disease and complications such as vaso-occlusive crisis, acute chest syndrome, and avascular necrosis of the femoral head. It also includes Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia/macroglobulinemia, which is unrelated to the other conditions. These reflect distinct, unrelated conditions rather than a single clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine disparate patterns: congenital cystic kidney disease with renal transplant sequelae, hidradenitis suppurativa, and myelodysplastic syndrome. Additional findings such as non‑healing wounds and various infections reinforce the absence of a single unifying clinical condition.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This grouping combines bipolar II disorder with plantar warts and scattered unrelated conditions (e.g., chlamydial infection, hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy, ovarian masses). There is no unifying clinical mechanism across these diagnoses, so it does not represent a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span invasive and in‑situ breast malignancy, benign breast disorders, imaging abnormalities, nodal involvement, postoperative state, and common comorbidities without a single unified clinical state. This reflects a broad, nonspecific breast pathology mix rather than a focused phenotype useful for selection.

phenotypes.label: Laryngeal cancer, bladder cancer, CLL
phenotypes.description: This combines laryngeal/glottic malignancy and voice disorders (dysphonia, aphonia, vocal cord paralysis) with urothelial bladder cancer and chronic lymphocytic leukemia. These represent distinct disease processes with no single unifying clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Retinal disease and inner ear disorders
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions include central/branch retinal vein occlusion, retinal edema, and central serous chorioretinopathy alongside Ménière’s disease with vertigo, sensorineural hearing loss, and otitis externa. These represent unrelated ophthalmic and otologic conditions rather than a single clinical pathway, so there is no unifying theme.

phenotypes.label: Cutaneous malignant melanoma
phenotypes.description: Primary skin cancers with strong emphasis on malignant melanoma, including site-specific melanoma of the lower limb, trunk, face, and scalp/neck. Autoimmune connective tissue conditions (dermatomyositis, systemic sclerosis, Raynaud phenomenon) and some sinonasal disorders appear in a subset, but the core signal is melanoma of the skin.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common chronic conditions seen across many adults, including hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD, obesity, mood and anxiety disorders, asthma, and musculoskeletal pain. Thyroid disorders are also represented, including hypothyroidism and thyroid malignancy. This reflects baseline comorbidity burden rather than a focused disease phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not share a single clinical theme. The list mixes unrelated vascular, peripheral nerve, autonomic, gastrointestinal, dermatologic, and hand musculoskeletal problems without a unifying pattern.

phenotypes.label: Cataract, glaucoma, and macular degeneration
phenotypes.description: Age-related ophthalmic conditions dominate: cataracts, primary open‑angle glaucoma/ocular hypertension, dry eye and blepharitis, vitreous degeneration and epiretinal membrane, and age‑related macular degeneration with neovascular changes. Refractive errors (myopia, presbyopia, astigmatism) and optic nerve/retinal pathology indicate ongoing ophthalmology follow-up in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Primary pancreatic head malignancy
phenotypes.description: Diagnoses center on primary malignant neoplasm of the pancreas, frequently specifying involvement of the pancreatic head. Nonspecific symptom codes (respiratory complaints, chills, vestibular/neurologic symptoms) co-occur and likely reflect presentations around the time of cancer diagnosis rather than a distinct condition.

phenotypes.label: Benign skin lesions and photodamage
phenotypes.description: Dermatologic conditions centered on actinic damage: actinic and seborrheic keratoses, solar lentigines, pigmentary changes, and multiple melanocytic nevi with benign cutaneous tumors. Also includes basal cell carcinoma and neoplasms of uncertain behavior on sun-exposed sites.

phenotypes.label: Childhood exanthems and herpesvirus infections
phenotypes.description: Viral exanthem cluster featuring varicella, measles, mumps, and rubella, alongside herpes simplex and herpesviral vesicular dermatitis. Represents patients coded with acute childhood-type viral rash illnesses and cutaneous herpesvirus infections, a pattern that can occur in adults, especially when immunity is incomplete or compromised.

phenotypes.label: Monoclonal gammopathy and myeloma
phenotypes.description: Plasma cell dyscrasias centered on monoclonal gammopathy and multiple myeloma, including solitary plasmacytoma. Associated complications include systemic or localized amyloidosis, anemia/cytopenias (including refractory anemia), autoimmune hemolytic anemia, and peripheral neuropathy.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not define a consistent, clinically interpretable pattern in this dataset. The signal is weak and not useful for defining a patient subgroup.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: No shared clinical theme across the leading conditions. Common baseline comorbidities appear alongside scattered, unrelated diagnoses, indicating no coherent clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and obstetric complications
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy and obstetric complications spanning all trimesters and the puerperium, with extensive gestational-age and high‑risk pregnancy coding. Conditions include placenta previa, premature labor with painful/irregular contractions, maternal obesity complicating pregnancy, unplanned or uncertain-viability pregnancies, peripartum mental disorders (postpartum depression), and associated gynecologic findings such as cervix examination, abnormal Pap smear, and vaginitis.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptoms and chronic comorbidities
phenotypes.description: General symptom presentations predominate—pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea/vomiting—alongside ubiquitous chronic conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, obesity, GERD, anxiety, and OSA. This reflects non‑specific, cross‑cutting clinical encounters rather than a single disease phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are unrelated, spanning ocular findings (exophthalmos, retinal lattice degeneration), vulvar/perineal disorders, and anorectal bleeding/hemorrhoids with scattered common symptoms. There is no unifying diagnosis tying these together, so this signal is not clinically interpretable for cohort selection.

phenotypes.label: Sexually active status
phenotypes.description: This cluster is driven by documentation of a patient being sexually active, a social/behavioral history element rather than a disease condition. The remaining conditions appear incidental and do not define a single clinical syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Heart transplant with infections/malignancy
phenotypes.description: Patients with a cardiac transplant and transplant-related complications. The pattern includes opportunistic fungal infections (histoplasmosis, aspergillosis) and secondary malignancies (soft‑tissue/trunk sarcomas, Hodgkin‑type lymphoid cancer), consistent with chronic immunosuppression; GI inflammatory disease and cholangitis also co-occur.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: No shared clinical theme across the leading conditions. The list mixes generic symptoms and common chronic comorbidities with scattered obstetric and unrelated findings, offering no coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Cardiometabolic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common chronic cardiometabolic multimorbidity: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes (with microvascular complications), obesity, obstructive sleep apnea, GERD, coronary atherosclerosis, stage 3 CKD, and atrial fibrillation. This reflects baseline chronic disease burden and general adult multimorbidity, not a single focused condition.

phenotypes.label: Psychiatric disorders and myeloid leukemia
phenotypes.description: Blend of depressive and anxiety-spectrum conditions (dysthymia, major depression, panic, ADHD, borderline personality) alongside chronic myeloid leukemia and other unrelated diagnoses such as ulcerative colitis, poisonings, and ocular injuries. These represent disjoint clinical themes without a unifying mechanism, so this is not a single interpretable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Bronchiectasis with NTM and Pseudomonas
phenotypes.description: Structural lung disease marked by bronchiectasis and cystic fibrosis, complicated by non‑tuberculous mycobacterial lung disease, Pseudomonas and staphylococcal colonization/infection, chronic sinusitis, cough, and hemoptysis. Frequent secondary diabetes and pancreatic disorders point to cystic fibrosis–related endocrine involvement; alpha‑1 antitrypsin deficiency and lymphocytopenia appear in a subset.

phenotypes.label: Endometrial and gynecologic malignancies
phenotypes.description: Pattern centered on endometrial (uterine corpus) cancer, with related gynecologic malignancies of the ovary, cervix, and fallopian tube. Co-occurring uterine and adnexal conditions (leiomyomas, endometrial polyps, endometriosis, ovarian cysts) and presentations such as postmenopausal bleeding, heavy menstrual bleeding, pelvic pain, and pelvic mass are prominent; obesity is a frequent comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Acute cardiopulmonary and GI symptoms
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute presentations with pain, chest pain, dyspnea/wheezing, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea or constipation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and anxiety/distress. This reflects general symptomatic encounters rather than a single disease process, spanning cardiopulmonary and gastrointestinal complaints common across patients with serious illness.

phenotypes.label: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension, HCC
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic liver disease with cirrhosis and portal hypertension, including esophageal varices, ascites, splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia, and hepatic encephalopathy. Etiologies include hepatitis C, alcohol-related liver disease, and NASH; some patients have hepatocellular carcinoma or a history of liver transplantation.

phenotypes.label: HIV with head/neck and anal cancer
phenotypes.description: Patients with HIV infection and concurrent HPV-spectrum malignancies of the head and neck and the anal canal (tongue, tonsil, oropharynx, anus). Associated conditions include neck masses with nodal metastases, dysphagia, salivary dysfunction, oral mucosal ulceration, and occasional Kaposi sarcoma or syphilis.

phenotypes.label: Brain tumors and corneal disease
phenotypes.description: This combines intracranial neoplasms (frontal lobe and other brain tumors) and related neurologic findings such as seizures, cerebral edema, visual field loss, and postoperative states with separate corneal pathology. The corneal ulcer and history of corneal transplant represent a distinct ocular surface disease process not mechanistically linked to the brain tumor pattern.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine generic symptoms (pain, bleeding, chest pain, nausea, shortness of breath) with unrelated ocular, shoulder, and miscellaneous diagnoses without a unifying clinical picture. This mixture lacks a coherent clinical theme and is not useful for identifying a specific patient phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis and cutaneous T-cell lymphoma
phenotypes.description: Dermatologic cluster featuring psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis alongside cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma/mycosis fungoides. Vulvar and vaginal malignancies and vulvar/perineal dermatoses also appear, indicating a mucocutaneous oncology theme within dermatology.
